# Polaris Interest Rates

> How Polaris uses issuance and redemption activity to set autonomous pAsset interest rates.

Canonical URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting/interest-rates
Markdown URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting/interest-rates.md
Section: Understand Polaris

Documentation index: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/llms.txt
Full documentation bundle: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/llms-full.txt

---

Polaris uses interest rates to help pAssets trade around peg.

The main rate is the peg-steering rate. It adjusts automatically and does not depend on discretionary governance decisions or direct market-price oracles.

This reflects a broader design choice throughout Polaris. Whenever a process can be handled by code, it is. When it cannot, incentives are used instead. Stewardship is used where neither approach is enough.

For interest rates, the automatable signal is pAsset peg-defense activity.

Issuance is the above-peg signal.

Redemptions are the below-peg signal.

The peg-steering rate responds to those flows.

## Why Interest Rates Matter

The interest rate is the cost paid by pAsset issuers.

The peg-steering rate responds to peg conditions rather than serving as a general benchmark for the cost of capital.

## The Peg Signal

Polaris does not need to ask an oracle whether USDp is trading at \$0.99 or \$1.01. Instead, it observes how users interact with the protocol.

Above peg: Arbitrageurs issue pAssets and sell them into the market. More issuance indicates stronger demand for that pAsset.

Below peg: Arbitrageurs buy discounted pAssets and redeem them for pETH collateral. More redemptions indicate that supply exceeds demand.

The protocol uses this onchain activity to drive the peg-steering rate.

## Peg-Steering Rate

The peg-steering rate is the primary interest rate paid by pAsset issuers.

It adjusts whenever pAssets are issued or redeemed. More issuance pushes the rate lower, while more redemptions push it higher.

This creates a feedback loop:

- A pAsset trades above peg.
- Issuance becomes profitable.
- Issuance expands supply.
- The peg-steering rate moves lower.
- Lower rates make new positions more attractive.
- Supply can expand until the opportunity closes and the pAsset returns toward peg.

And in the other direction:

- A pAsset trades below peg.
- Redemptions become profitable.
- Redemptions contract supply.
- The peg-steering rate moves higher.
- Higher rates make issued debt more expensive.
- Supply can tighten until the opportunity closes and the pAsset returns toward peg.

## Why This Is Novel

This mechanism exists because Polaris has permissionless issuance and redemptions.

Many collateralized debt systems can only react through governance, external market-price feeds, or manual parameter changes.

Polaris can observe issuance and redemption activity directly, then use that activity to adjust rates automatically.

This allows the rate to reflect conditions inside the protocol as they evolve, with no offchain input.

## Accrual

The peg-steering rate is continuously added to an issuer's outstanding debt.

This means the cost of an open position grows over time while debt remains outstanding.

Issuers should monitor:

- The current peg-steering rate.
- Their outstanding debt.
- Their LTV.
- Their liquidation price.

## Bounds

The peg-steering rate is autonomous and not directly controlled by stewards.

The expected minimum is zero. Final production bounds and implementation details should be verified in [Parameters](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet#parameters) and reviewed against the deployed contracts.

## Spike-And-Decay Fees

Issuance and redemptions also use spike-and-decay fees.

These fees make large sudden flows more expensive and help prevent issuance and redemptions from being used as a low-cost way to influence the rate.

When peg-defense volume spikes, fees rise.

When peg-defense volume slows, fees decay.

## Safety Rate

The safety rate is the secondary interest rate within Polaris. It remains inactive under normal conditions and only comes into play during stressed conditions.

The safety rate activates once a pAsset market falls below its TCR threshold.

On current testnet, this threshold is 165%, but final values should be verified in [Parameters](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet#parameters).

When active, the safety rate moves value from riskier positions to healthier positions.

Higher-LTV issuers pay the safety rate.

Lower-LTV issuers receive it.

This encourages collateral-only and low-leverage positions to enter the system, earn the additional rate, and improve pAsset market health.

At the same time, the safety rate penalizes excessive leverage when the system is already under stress.

Final safety-rate parameters, activation conditions, and display logic should be checked in [Parameters](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet#parameters) once published.

## Where Interest Goes

Peg-steering interest is protocol revenue.

Depending on the pAsset market and configured splits, it can flow to:

- Earn Vault depositors.
- vePOLAR lockers.
- Ecosystem or Flow paths where configured.

Once live, vePOLAR is designed to receive a guaranteed minimum share of issuer interest from deployed pAsset markets.

Safety-rate payments are different: they move value directly between issuers rather than to these destinations.

Current splits and pending production values live in [Parameters](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet#parameters).

## What Issuers Should Watch

- Interest is continuously added to outstanding debt.
- The peg-steering rate can move when issuance or redemptions occur.
- Spike-and-decay fees can make heavy issuance or redemption activity more expensive.
- The safety rate can add cost to higher-LTV positions during stressed conditions.
- Lower-LTV positions can receive safety-rate payments when the safety rate is active.
- Current values should be checked in the app and in [Parameters](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/testnet#parameters).
